resposta:
solution:
```
class Solution:
    def maxSubstrings(self, word: str) -> int:
        n = len(word)
        dp = [0] * n
        for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            if i + 3 < n and word[i] == word[i + 3]:
                dp[i] = max(dp[i + 4] + 1, dp[i + 1])
            else:
                dp[i] = dp[i + 1]
        return dp[0]
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses dynamic programming to build up a table of maximum non-intersecting substrings for each prefix of the input string.
